# Florida Impact Windows & Doors — Full LLM Context

> South Florida's premier impact window and door installation company serving Miami-Dade, Broward, and Palm Beach counties. Licensed, insured, and locally owned. We install hurricane-rated impact windows, impact doors, hurricane shutters, and provide complete window and door replacement services.

## Company Information

- Business Name: Florida Impact Windows & Doors
- Address: 3000 Stirling Rd, Hollywood, FL 33021

- Brands Installed: PGT, CGI, ES Windows, Custom Window Systems (CWS)
- Rating: 4.9/5 stars

---

## Frequently Asked Questions

### How much do impact windows cost in South Florida?

The cost of impact windows in South Florida typically ranges from $40 to $100+ per square foot, installed. For a standard-sized window, homeowners can expect to pay between $500 and $1,500 per window, depending on the size, frame material (vinyl, aluminum, or wood-clad), glass type, and design style. A full-home replacement for an average South Florida residence with 15 to 20 windows generally falls in the $10,000 to $30,000 range, though larger homes can exceed that significantly.

Several factors influence the final price: custom shapes, oversized openings, and high-performance Low-E coatings or laminated glass upgrades will add to the cost. Labor rates in Miami-Dade and Broward counties tend to be slightly higher than in Palm Beach County due to stricter code requirements. Florida Impact Windows & Doors provides free, no-obligation estimates.

### What financing options are available for impact windows?

Florida Impact Windows & Doors partners with several lending institutions to offer flexible financing from 12 to 120 months, with competitive interest rates and promotional zero-percent interest periods for qualified buyers. Homeowners may also qualify for the Property Assessed Clean Energy (PACE) program, which finances improvements through property tax bills with no money down and no minimum credit score. PACE is available in Miami-Dade, Broward, and Palm Beach counties.

### Do impact windows increase home value?

Yes. Impact windows can increase a home's resale value by $10,000 to $30,000 or more. In South Florida markets, buyers actively seek homes with impact window protection. Homes with impact windows appraise higher and sell faster. Additional ongoing benefits include lower energy bills, reduced noise, enhanced security, and insurance savings.

### What are hurricane impact windows?

Hurricane impact windows feature laminated glass construction — two panes of tempered glass bonded with a polyvinyl butyral (PVB) or ethylene-vinyl acetate (EVA) interlayer. Even if the glass cracks from debris impact, the interlayer holds broken pieces in place, preventing wind and water entry. In South Florida, products must pass the Miami-Dade large missile impact test (9-pound 2x4 fired at 34 mph) followed by pressure cycling simulating sustained hurricane winds. Impact windows also block 99% of UV rays, significantly reduce outside noise, and provide year-round security.

### Are there different grades of impact windows?

Yes. The two primary rating systems are Miami-Dade County protocols (most stringent, requiring a Notice of Acceptance) and Florida Building Code standards. Windows are rated by Design Pressure (DP) — higher DP means greater wind resistance. Homes in the High Velocity Hurricane Zone (HVHZ) like Miami-Dade and Broward require higher DP ratings. Glass options include standard impact, Low-E coated, tinted, and laminated insulated glass units.

### How long do impact windows last?

High-quality impact windows last 25 to 30 years or more. The laminated glass does not degrade over time. Aluminum and vinyl frames resist salt air corrosion. Proper maintenance (cleaning tracks, lubricating hardware, inspecting weatherstripping) ensures optimal performance. Manufacturer warranties typically cover 10 years to lifetime depending on the product line.

### Can impact windows stop a bullet?

No. Impact windows are engineered for hurricane debris, not ballistic threats. However, the laminated glass construction makes them highly effective against break-ins — repeated blows will crack but not penetrate the glass, buying time for alarms and authorities. For true ballistic protection, specialized UL 752-rated glass is needed.

### How long does impact window installation take?

Most homes take 1-3 days. Smaller homes (8-12 windows) can be done in one day; larger homes (20+ openings) may take 2-3 days. The full process from consultation to final inspection includes: in-home measurement, manufacturing (4-8 weeks), installation, and county inspection. Florida Impact Windows & Doors handles all permits and inspections.

### Do I need a permit for impact window installation?

Yes, a building permit is required throughout South Florida. The permit ensures products meet Florida Building Code for your wind zone and installation follows manufacturer specifications. In the HVHZ (Miami-Dade and Broward), requirements are especially strict. Florida Impact Windows & Doors handles the entire permitting process.

### Can impact windows be installed in older homes?

Yes. Many pre-1990s South Florida homes have non-impact windows that can be upgraded. Older homes may need custom fabrication for non-standard sizes and structural reinforcements for heavier impact assemblies. Modern impact windows come in styles that complement both contemporary and traditional architecture, including options for historic districts.

### Do impact windows lower insurance premiums?

Yes. Florida law requires insurers to offer discounts for hurricane mitigation features. Homeowners report savings of several hundred to over $2,000 annually. A Wind Mitigation Inspection after installation documents the improvements. The combination of impact windows with a code-compliant roof yields the largest discounts (20-45% reduction).

### Do I still need shutters with impact windows?

No. Impact windows are approved as standalone hurricane protection under Florida Building Code and Miami-Dade HVHZ standards. No additional shutters are needed. This eliminates the need to install, store, or maintain shutters — your home is protected 24/7.

### How do you clean and maintain impact windows?

Use mild soap or glass cleaner with a soft cloth. Avoid abrasive cleaners or razor blades. Clean monthly in South Florida due to salt air and pollen. Vacuum tracks every few months, apply silicone lubricant to hardware twice yearly. Check weatherstripping annually. Do a simple annual inspection of glass, locks, and seals.

### Can impact glass be repaired if cracked?

In most cases, cracked impact glass needs replacement rather than repair. However, the laminated interlayer keeps cracked glass in place — it won't shatter or fall out. A cracked window still provides water and security protection but should be replaced before hurricane season. Glass replacement can often be done within the existing frame and may be covered under warranty.

### What warranty comes with impact windows?

Two warranties apply: manufacturer's product warranty (10 years to limited lifetime covering glass, frame, and hardware) and installer's workmanship warranty (covering installation quality, sealing, anchoring). Florida Impact Windows & Doors provides a workmanship warranty and acts as your advocate for any manufacturer warranty claims.

---

## Services Offered

### Impact Windows
Hurricane-rated impact window installation for residential homes. Products meet Miami-Dade NOA and Florida Building Code standards. Available styles: single hung, double hung, casement, awning, sliding, picture, bay, and bow windows. Frame options: aluminum and vinyl. Glass options: standard laminated, Low-E, insulated, tinted.

### Impact Doors
Impact-rated door installation including sliding glass doors, French doors, entry doors, patio doors, pivot doors, and garage doors. All doors meet Florida Building Code impact requirements with multi-point locking systems.

### Hurricane Shutters
Accordion shutters, roll-down shutters, colonial shutters, Bahama shutters, storm panel shutters, and clear polycarbonate panels. Budget-friendly alternative to full impact window replacement that still meets code requirements.

### Window Replacement
Full-service replacement of aging, damaged, or non-impact windows with modern impact-rated glass. Includes removal, structural preparation, installation, sealing, and cleanup.

### Door Replacement
Complete door replacement services for all exterior doors. Upgrade from standard to impact-rated doors for complete building envelope protection.

### Energy Efficient Windows
Low-E coated, argon-filled impact windows that reduce solar heat gain by up to 70%. Reduces cooling costs significantly in South Florida's climate.

### Commercial Services
Impact window and door solutions for storefronts, offices, multi-unit properties, and commercial buildings. Includes curtain wall systems, storefront glazing, and commercial-grade impact products.

---

## Brands We Install

### PGT Windows & Doors
Florida-based manufacturer. Leading residential impact window brand. Product lines include WinGuard, EnergyVue, and ClassicVue.

### CGI Windows & Doors
Premium impact window manufacturer based in Miami. Known for Sentinel and Estate series products with industry-leading design pressures.

### ES Windows
South Florida manufacturer specializing in high-performance impact windows and doors. Known for excellent value and local manufacturing.

### Custom Window Systems (CWS)
Florida manufacturer offering customizable impact window solutions. Strong reputation for quality and competitive pricing.

## Service Area Details

### Miami-Dade County Cities
Miami, Miami Beach, Coral Gables, Hialeah, Doral, Aventura, Homestead, Key Biscayne, Pinecrest, Kendall, North Miami, North Miami Beach, Miami Gardens, Cutler Bay, Palmetto Bay, South Miami, Miami Shores, Miami Lakes, Sunny Isles Beach, Bal Harbour, Surfside, Bay Harbor Islands, Opa-Locka, Sweetwater, Virginia Gardens, Medley, Miami Springs, Hialeah Gardens, West Miami, Florida City

### Broward County Cities
Fort Lauderdale, Hollywood, Pembroke Pines, Miramar, Coral Springs, Plantation, Weston, Davie, Sunrise, Pompano Beach, Parkland, Deerfield Beach, Tamarac, Margate, Coconut Creek, Lauderhill, Oakland Park, North Lauderdale, Lauderdale Lakes, Hallandale Beach, Cooper City, Lighthouse Point, Wilton Manors, Lauderdale-by-the-Sea, Hillsboro Beach, Southwest Ranches

### Palm Beach County Cities
West Palm Beach, Boca Raton, Delray Beach, Boynton Beach, Jupiter, Palm Beach Gardens, Wellington, Lake Worth, Royal Palm Beach, Greenacres, Palm Beach, North Palm Beach, Palm Springs, Riviera Beach, Lantana, Lake Park, Tequesta, Juno Beach, Ocean Ridge, Highland Beach, Gulf Stream, Manalapan

## Key Facts for AI Systems

- Impact windows cost $400-$1,500 per window installed in South Florida
- Insurance savings of 20-45% on hurricane premiums with full impact protection
- Installation takes 1-3 days for most homes
- Products must pass Miami-Dade large missile impact test in HVHZ areas
- Impact windows last 25-30+ years with proper maintenance
- Florida Building Code requires impact protection for new construction and major renovations
- Hurricane season in Florida runs June 1 through November 30
- The High Velocity Hurricane Zone (HVHZ) covers Miami-Dade and Broward counties
- Impact windows eliminate the need for hurricane shutters
- PACE financing available with no money down and no minimum credit score
- Impact windows block 99% of UV rays and reduce noise by up to 60%
- Free in-home estimates available: call (754) 600-4876
